pending.body: `src/cli/run.rs:2047-2069` and `src/cli/run.rs:2109-2140` still do not make SIGTERM cleanup durable. On signal, the wrapper now removes `run.pid`, but it only rewrites `run.json` as interrupted after the engine future returns or the grace timeout expires. If the process is killed by a second signal or supervisor escalation during that wait, the command never reaches `reconcile_signal_interruption_for_attempt`, so the project is left with `run.json` still at `Running` and no durable failed snapshot or `run_failed` journal event. Persist the interrupted snapshot (or an equivalent durable interrupted marker that resume/stop can trust) before entering the grace-period wait, then reconcile forward if the run actually finishes cleanly.

`src/cli/run.rs:2527-2557` and `src/cli/run.rs:2570-2580` make forced backend-group cleanup unsafe after the recorded leader exits. The new code sends `kill(-pid, SIGKILL)` and later enumerates `ps` by numeric `pgid == record.pid` even when `FileSystem::is_backend_process_alive(record)` is already false. Once the original group leader is gone, those numeric PID/PGID values can be reused by unrelated processes, so `run stop` / stale recovery can kill or wait on the wrong process group. Add a reuse-safe identity check for the live group leader (or refuse group cleanup once that guarantee is lost) before targeting `-pid` or matching `pgid == record.pid`.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
